description: Set up the vanilla JS + Canvas project scaffolding with an HTML page, a <canvas> element, a game loop running at 60fps via requestAnimationFrame, and a tile-based coordinate system. This is the foundation every other system builds on.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user opens index.html in a browser, when the page loads, then a full-window <canvas> element is displayed with a black background.
A game loop runs via requestAnimationFrame at a stable 60fps, with a delta-time value available to all update functions.
A 2D grid coordinate system is implemented where each tile is 32x32 pixels.
The canvas renders a visible grid of at least 25x17 tiles at 1080p resolution.
A simple camera object tracks a position and can be used to offset all rendering calls.
The project consists of index.html, a main JS entry point, and a modular file structure (e.g., src/engine/, src/game/) loadable via ES modules or a simple bundler.
spec_section: 9. Platform & Tech Strategy, A.2.1 Perspective & Tile Size
notes: Tech stack override: spec recommended Godot but project will use vanilla JS + Canvas HTML for easy browser launch. 32x32 tile size per spec Appendix A.2.1.
